name: John Mendoza-Garcia
summary: John Mendoza-Garcia is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.9/5 in overall quality and 2.7/5 in difficulty across 160 review(s). 45% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: You'll love this course if you were a fan of the teacher who assigned homework just cause in high school. This course is such a genuine waste of time it is ridiculous. Grading is entirely based upon your TA, and JMG spends most lecture videos showing you the wrong way to do something then saying "now do this instead" at minute 27. Take ISM.

1/3 of the time spent on assignments is actually trying to understand the instructions & what you're supposed to do. In his demo videos he'll give you three wrong ways to do it or show you the way you could do it but aren't gonna actually perform the action before he shows you what you're really supposed to do. Go in w patience and you'll survive.

He cares about his students but is not a good teacher. When taking this class you are basically entirely on your own. He doesn't explain the meanings of the assignments, and the videos he gives for "help" are just him mumbling and messing up in Excel. He edits the videos when he messes up. I knew excel prior, if you don't then don't take this class

name: Marvin Andujar
summary: Marvin Andujar is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 1.3/5 in overall quality and 4.7/5 in difficulty across 43 review(s). 2% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: It's like swimming in a pool full of cement.
Paid $3000 to watch youtube videos
¡Mi abuela enseña mil veces mejor que él!

name: Michael Link
summary: Michael Link is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.7/5 in overall quality and 3.5/5 in difficulty across 41 review(s). 71% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Professor Link seems well-meaning, giving extra credit, extending deadlines, bringing food, and being active on Discord, but the class is chaotic and stressful. Exams run past midnight, content is overloaded, grading is extremely slow, instructions constantly change, is constantly fear mongering about AI, and students are left constantly anxious.

He cares, but he comes to class under-prepared. The exams and labs are broken to the point of it being detrimental to the class. His fearmongering is worse than Professor Schwartz, The lecture material does not prepare you for the exams, There are people who display culty behavior when talking about him. Pls avoid his class unless u knw wht u doin.
Worst professor I've ever had and bizarre that he's in the Don files texting Ep about his thoughts on teaching and making an analogy to a certain performance.

name: Cheryl Resch
summary: Cheryl Resch is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.6/5 in overall quality and 2.4/5 in difficulty across 119 review(s). 76% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: One of my top 3 professors I've taken at UF. Professor Resch may be slightly passive aggressive if you say something out of pocket or if she has to keep repeating herself, but she is one of the most actually caring professors I have ever taken a class with. She kind of feels like a new yorker, she might be passive at times, but she genuinely cares.

Though I didn't love all the content in this class, Dr. Resch made the experience significantly better. She is organized, passionate, funny, and makes it easy to grasp difficult/boring concepts. The Canvas is very organized, exams are straightforward, and projects are perfectly manageable (very easy to get help on Discord and in office hours).

prof reach is such a queen, we stan her.

easy examlets. assembly projects were hard but graded leniently. mandatory attendance occasionally

name: Amanpreet Kapoor
summary: Amanpreet Kapoor is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.8/5 in overall quality and 3.2/5 in difficulty across 106 review(s). 96% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Best prof in the computer science department. Very clear on theory, great visualizations to help you follow along. Incredibly well prepared, organized class structure. Gamified lecture quizzes for extra credit. Responsive to questions. Open to feedback. Developed an arguably better version of leetcode for students. Actually the GOAT.

Incredible professor. Enthusiastic, clear, and accessible. Large network of TAs and well-crafted Canvas make this the most organized course I've ever taken and the CS class (a) I've learned the most in and (b) I've had the most fun in. Consistent quizzes help with studying and make exam prep easier. 3 fun and fair projects. Plenty of extra credit.

Aman is helpful and a good lecturer, but his class is very hard because his quizzes and exams feel like they are there to trick you. He provides an abundance of review material only for almost none of that to be tested. He will mainly test you on niche details of the topic that were barely or never touched on in discussion and lecture.

name: Tamer Kahveci
summary: Tamer Kahveci is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.4/5 in overall quality and 2.8/5 in difficulty across 31 review(s). 78% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: He is the best

He is clear about his expectations from the students. The content taught by him is useful in the field of computer science in general as well, as the algorithms he teaches in class are generally applicable in other problems of computer science. Has a really good sense of humor, and keeps the lecture fun and interesting throughout.
Thank you for being you

name: Selwyn Piramuthu
summary: Selwyn Piramuthu is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.2/5 in overall quality and 1.8/5 in difficulty across 17 review(s). 88% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Book was useless; don't buy it. Selwyn is very nice and a charismatic lecturer, but all we did in this class was draw diagrams, usually with no real-world context. Both exams are 3 questions of reading, interpreting, and drawing a diagram. That's it. Since drawing diagrams can be ambiguous, grading is subjective, but he tends to be an easy grader.

I would echo the comments about Dr. Piramuthu being quite energetic - does a decent job in his presentation of material, but sometimes assumes that students have knowledge that many don't.  Overall, a fun and informative class.
He is the GOAT. Explains the content clearly and concisely on top of that he is a comedic professor that does his job without wasting time. He will not disappoint.

name: Ashish Aggarwal
summary: Ashish Aggarwal is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.5/5 in overall quality and 2.4/5 in difficulty across 74 review(s). 92% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: He did a great job teaching MATLAB and showing students how to problem solve and think outside of the box. Office hours are always available and the TA's will be a tremendous help with any trouble you're having. The exams are fair and the final project is fun and rewarding. I loved the class.

Amazing Professor! He genuinely cares about his students and goes out of his way to make sure that you are understanding the material and doing well in the class. He explains things thoroughly and makes sure you grasp the concepts and are prepared for the exams. He will stay after class to help you and he has a lot of office hours!

This is the best professor at UF. Even if youre not engineering, you should still bear witness to world class teaching. He will explain every detail of every line. No magic tricks. This is teaching on a level I have never seen before.

name: Peter Dobbins
summary: Peter Dobbins is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.4/5 in overall quality and 3.2/5 in difficulty across 252 review(s). 31% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: HORRIBLE lecturer. Lectures are disorganized and go off-topic. Pre-lecture videos are unhelpful. Exams are worth 70% and rarely reflect practice problems or what's covered in class. You'll be teaching yourself through the textbook and YouTube. Nice guy, but not effective at teaching. Take a different professor if possible.

Get ready to attend YouTube university with this class. Like honestly, not a single soul actually learns enough from lectures alone to prepare us enough for the exams that's worth 70% of our grade. I like Peter, but this class can't be like this.

This class is entirely self taught. Dobbins knows what he is talking about, but has some of the worst teaching ever so you will only be reading from textbook and watching youtube videos. Exams are also 70% of your grade.

name: Marko  Suvajdzic
summary: Marko  Suvajdzic is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4/5 in overall quality and 2/5 in difficulty across 13 review(s). 85% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Pretty solid. Class wasn't structured super well and the book was never used (although, it actually looks like an interesting read so maybe still get it, anyway). Tons of back-to-back group projects, but nothing too difficult. Helps if you're artistic. Actually talks about gaming industry which is probably more helpful than the stuff in class.

Marko was very passionate about class topics and was available for us to ask questions. His lectures were always interesting and he encouraged a great learning environment that kept us engaged. Instructions and due dates were always clear and properly communicated.

I took his course in Fall 2021 and I felt his priorities were rather odd. He's more of a business person than a programming person, and the lectures reflected this, when they could have been used to help reinforce learning Unity and C# as well as art programs. Still a worthwhile experience.

name: Lisha Zhou
summary: Lisha Zhou is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.5/5 in overall quality and 3.2/5 in difficulty across 108 review(s). 64% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: If you have no coding experience, its HARD. Very very hard. I was required to take this for DAS and had no interest in programming but was willing to learn. I went to lectures for the first month or so then realized it didn't help all that much. She seemed nice, but classes were majorly unengaging. She does offer EC for zybooks so that's cool.

I didn't rely much upon her teaching abilities, but the class structure is excellent. The projects and labs blend together the material quite well. As long as you are able to keep up it is manageable and even easy. Plenty of extra credit.

Prof. Zhou is fantastic. I thought the course was very well structured and she taught everything with crystal clear clarity. I really enjoyed learning more about Python and as a first time programmer appreciated how in depth everything was. All quizzes, exams, and labs were very fair. This is by far the best intro level course I've taken at UF.

name: Alper Ungor
summary: Alper Ungor is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.5/5 in overall quality and 3.9/5 in difficulty across 103 review(s). 56% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Dr. Ungor is a great professor. He uses powerpoint slides as a base, but expands on them a lot so it is very helpful to go to class. Very clear in his explanations and helpful. If you do the homework and understand it tests aren't that bad. Definitely recommended!

He spent a ton of time going over the easy concepts and flew through the hard stuff. His lectures are straight from the textbook (examples are even the same) and the discussions do not help at all. I would avoid taking this class with him.

Great teacher, hard exams. he's one of the most professional lecturers I've had, he comes to class prepared. The homework isn't for a grade but you wont pass unless you do it well and understand the concepts.

name: Alexandre Gomes de Siqueira
summary: Alexandre Gomes de Siqueira is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.2/5 in overall quality and 3.9/5 in difficulty across 74 review(s). 57% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Unbelievably terrible. Projects are near impossible to do without working in groups. Projects consist of 60+ hours of googling trying to understand what the project actually should be. Assignments are described as "open ended" but all require a single method of solution that can only be figured out after running thousands of test cases.

This was single-handedly the hardest and most infuriating course I've ever taken at UF. The professor made the course way more difficult this semester by not dropping any of the projects when he had done so in the previous semesters. His lecture content was not helpful for the projects at all. Cannot stress enough how miserable he made this course.

Projects and exercises are impossibly difficult,barely relate to the material and he does not go over the projects or even explains them. You're using a custom version of linux and manipulating the kernel.Youre basically on your own for these assignments which are a huge part of your grade. Prepared to spend 30+ hours on assingments.

name: Jeremiah Blanchard
summary: Jeremiah Blanchard is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4/5 in overall quality and 3.8/5 in difficulty across 94 review(s). 73% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: He is really cares about the students in his class. The class I had him with had 500+ students and he would respond individually to everyone. He is very active on the class Slack and would answer questions is a timely manner.

Great professor and an even greater person. He truly cares about his students. The class was easy if you actually read the online books and studied. His exams are difficult, but he gives plenty of extra-credit to balance it out. If you put in the work you will easily get an A in the class.
Awesome professor. Helps you whenever you need and loves his job! He always encourages us to go outside our confort zone and become better programmers! 10/10

name: Jonathan Kavalan
summary: Jonathan Kavalan is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.5/5 in overall quality and 1.8/5 in difficulty across 18 review(s). 44% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Posting this mainly because he changed his last name last year from Liu to kavalan so anyone who wants true reviews on this teacher check out Jonathan Liu on here. This professor is the worst Ive ever had, minimal regard for students learning experience and lectures are completely useless.
I don't know what you guys are going to say but let's be real here for a second. Changing your name to avoid sh,tty review is a 300 iq move right there. You could all take a page from his book.

We cover about 1 slide every 20 minutes. 1 minute is spent on the slide, 19 minutes are spent telling stories. Useless stories. Started out with 440 kids in lecture. By the third week, we had 70. That's a 17% retention rate. Save yourself.

name: Christina Boucher
summary: Christina Boucher is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.3/5 in overall quality and 3.9/5 in difficulty across 41 review(s). 56% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: One of the worst teachers Ive ever had the misfortune of having. Reads slides like an audiobook. Tests cover two or more chapters every two weeks. Lets us assume that everything will be on the test when half isnt. Cant trust what she says is important to know. Furthermore, shes unaccommodating to students and doesnt try to pretend to care.

Her class was NOT impossible whatsoever. She covers material in the textbook during lecture. Read the textbook!! It is so helpful, she has one to supplement the course for a reason. No she is not evil, and many of the test questions were right from the slides. If you are actually passionate about computer science, this class is rewarding!
Just the worst teacher ever

name: Prabhat Mishra
summary: Prabhat Mishra is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.1/5 in overall quality and 3.5/5 in difficulty across 40 review(s). 41% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: He is a great professor, probably the best among the ones I have taken so far. I am actually pleasantly surprised because I saw few negative comments and was little concerned about taking this class, but my experience is exactly the opposite.

A great professor with strong academic background and knows what he teaches. His lectures are mainly based on his PowerPoint slides but he likes teaching on whiteboard as well. His exams are not so easy and you will find the time a bit short. Keep up with the course materials and read the book. Go to office hours if necessary. You will learn a lot.

I don't think Dr. Mishra was as bad as people make him seem. Although I don't agree with some of his policies, I would take him again. It definitely bugged me that attendance was mandatory and that grading was all on the curve with a set percentage, i.e. A (10% of class), etc. Otherwise, the lectures were enjoyable and he is very approachable.

name: Laura Cruz Castro
summary: Laura Cruz Castro is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.5/5 in overall quality and 3/5 in difficulty across 58 review(s). 91% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Be prepared for the big learning curve if you're coming from Python. While learning C++ got better with time, this professor was not helpful in applying what we learned. You will have to worry more about reading through her typos on an exam, or how to write the same answers to a survey every month. Her mistakes cause project revisions every week.

Professor Cruz definitely cared about us, but the structure of the class was very messy and the lectures weren't usually very helpful. The exams were absurdly hard and disorganized, the weekly quizzes were unnecessarily difficult, and the pacing of assignments was awful at times. This class will sadly be a struggle unless you are a coding prodigy.

She is a really good teacher I feel like a lot of the people reviewing her aren't CS majors and were expecting an easy class but this is the class before data structures. I started learning C++ at the beginning of the semester and did fine. Start on the assignments early and don't ask a TA for every little thing that you could google.

name: Bonnie Dorr
summary: Bonnie Dorr is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.9/5 in overall quality and 3.1/5 in difficulty across 11 review(s). 73% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Dr. Dorr's class is extremely valuable. She is extremely competent and has been doing research for decades. It can be hard to reach her outside of class, but the TAs are very available. If you study, you'll learn everything you need to know.

Every assignment/exam is open-everything so its ez to pass with a good grade. Projects are kinda pointless but considering most special topic courses are always full, this is a great alternative. Slides werent downloadable which was kinda a dealbreaker for taking notes but its wtv considering how free this class was

Very chill class, easy assignments and projects, and Dr. Dorr is very passionate and knowledgable about NLP. This semester she implemented BLADE which is an AI chatbot specific to the class and it helped a lot. The lectures/quizzes/exams are straightforward with no surprises.

name: Lisa Anthony
summary: Lisa Anthony is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.3/5 in overall quality and 3.6/5 in difficulty across 8 review(s). 29% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: El Diablo

Does not accept responsibility for her own mistakes (ex. ambiguous quiz questions) and penalizes students as a result. Feedback is provided, however, some comments do not make any sense. Super nitpicky, there seems to only be one way to complete a task in her eyes which is not always made clear and hurts creativity. Not good for a design class.

Rolls her eyes at students when they go up to her to ask a question about an in class assignment. Does not answer email or posts about grading concerns. Has zero respect for students in her class. Do yourself a favor and take a different course.

name: Edwin Marte
summary: Edwin Marte is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.5/5 in overall quality and 2/5 in difficulty across 25 review(s). 64% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: 100% would recommend. Very kind professor who wants to see you grow. Gives lots of extra credit that helps u boost ur grade. Exams are very fair

Professor Marte was great. He is very helpful with debugging during class or in office hours. The class work and practice exams prepare you well, and he cares about the success of his students. In MATLAB, once you understand the thought-process for solving problems, the rest of the class is easy.

This professor barely understand half the content he was supposed to teach, ended up having to figure everything out on my own through the Internet or helpful TAs. There were discrepancies between how he said things would be graded and how TAs graded it. Horrible professor but I did learn some cool electrical engineering basics.

name: Fatemeh Tavassoli
summary: Fatemeh Tavassoli is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.6/5 in overall quality and 3.2/5 in difficulty across 129 review(s). 69% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: I feel so bad reading all these bad reviews. She's super lenient compared to other instructors in CISE, gave lots of extra credit that can basically boost you up a whole letter grade. Her lectures aren't the most engaging, and the auto-graded exams can be finicky, but exam questions stick to the materials that you should know. SHE'S NOT THAT BAD.

As a girl, I really like her attitude.
But she needs to put herself on our shoes .
We are just a beginner. Also You need to study on your own .
I found her available out of the class too.

The quality of the lectures are not good and the class needs to be structured differently.  Also, my TA absolutely did not care to help me out on anything and would also act very unprofessionally and use profane language in class when he didn't want to explain a concept.  The midterms are very difficult and offer little to no partial credit.

name: Christina Gardner-McCune
summary: Christina Gardner-McCune is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 1.2/5 in overall quality and 3.8/5 in difficulty across 99 review(s). 4% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Irresponsible and disorganized. Opening on day 1 with an address to her RMP score... "This is because the class changes professors all the time". She won't take responsibility. Not only are her lectures low in quality, they also contain fundamental mistakes. She literally does not know the content. W/o her charisma her rmp would be closer to a 1.

All of the problems in the older posts still persists. Late to class nearly every single time (one time showing up 25 minutes late). No TA's until week 5 of the semester. Every single slide has not been updated / has multiple errors. Assignments keep getting delayed / need updating. Suffice to say, teaching this class is not her priority.

Doesn't grade anything. Doesn't answer technical questions. Terribly organized. expectations not clear. Terrible communication with students. Doesn't even bother to fix errors in required submissions.

name: Patrick Traynor
summary: Patrick Traynor is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.8/5 in overall quality and 3.5/5 in difficulty across 10 review(s). 90% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: CNT5410 is HARD mainly because it is designed for graduate students.  The exams and 2nd Assignment is difficult. Every lecture has a reading which must be done before class. Take this if you are good at Networking and interested in Security. DO NOT take this course as a third wheel, it will kill your GPA.

Prof. Traynor is an incredible lecturer and he always nurtures a healthy interaction with the students, both in and outside of the class. His lectures are fun and cover a lot of content, while the assigned reading gives you the details and depth you need.

Professor Traynor is probably one of the best CISE professors. You can tell he works really hard on his lectures because they are very engaging and well organized. The second assignment is as hard as this class gets. Personally, I always skip readings for classes but there's no way you can get away with that for this class. Great class overall.

name: Arunava Banerjee
summary: Arunava Banerjee is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.4/5 in overall quality and 3.4/5 in difficulty across 36 review(s). 93% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Math (in all its forms) has never tasted so good. Few have Prof. Arunava Banerjee's ability to present the hardest material in the easiest form just so that we have a clear picture and understanding of what is going on.

Here's how you judge whether you should take a graduate class or not :- Uses black board  Knows how to teach and cares if you are learning. (ADD) Uses presentation slides  Lazy and needs to finish the syllabus quickly,you would learn better by reading the textbook yourself. (DROP)
You may not find a teacher with his capability of rephrasing concepts again and again just so that you understand. (Do you want to waste your time taking classes under anybody else? I wouldn't. )

name: Joshua Fox
summary: Joshua Fox is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.3/5 in overall quality and 3.3/5 in difficulty across 91 review(s). 82% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Great lecturer who has very applicable knowledge to the real world... this wasn't just a C++ class, he really went in depth in teaching HOW to program with many different aspects of the science. Quizzes were easy, exams were doable, and extra credit was offered. The big time sinks were labs and projects -- starting early helped a lot.

Mr Fox might be the best teacher I've ever encountered. His lectures are great and the way the class is laid out is perfect for the material. Start early on the labs and especially the projects and reach out to your TA if you need help and you should do fine.
Too many gotchas on the quiz and exams. And is it entirely necessary to have tons of "look through the code and type what it outputs"? It's just plain busy work after 1.

name: Sharon Chu
summary: Sharon Chu is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 1.5/5 in overall quality and 4.3/5 in difficulty across 8 review(s). 12% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: She is very disorganized and has high weekly expectations. The project is 50% of the final grade with many submissions, but all of the grading is done after finals. She is extremely nitpicky about formatting, and late feedback prevents students from correcting mistakes. The class feels hectic, and I recommend taking it with another professor.

If you're able to take UX Design with anyone else, do it. It's supposed to be a great class but Sharon makes it quite intolerable. She seems like the kind of professor that truly doesn't want to teach and this reflects heavily in how she responds to students and how poorly organized the course is. I recommend waiting for Lok, Anthony, etc.
Never take her class

name: William Anderson
summary: William Anderson is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.7/5 in overall quality and 2.7/5 in difficulty across 7 review(s). 86% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Blake is goated. I did not ever attend the classes in person but even the recorded lectures felt very engaging. He is understanding and incredibly knowledgeable. He is also very easy going about extensions and personal circumstances. Quick turnaround for project questions; just beware that quizzes tend to be a bit hard and u may run out of time.

super smart and funny but class felt very unorganized with deadlines always shifting. frequent changes to assignments close to deadline. he's also very reasonable and understanding since he was a student not too long ago.

Blake is one of the best professors I've had here at UF. I came into this class just hoping to get by and not put a lot of effort in, only taking it because the CS degree required it. However, I've grown to love the content and Blake does a great job at teaching it. He's very smart, responds fast to questions/slack/emails, and is understanding :)

name: Sara Rampazzi
summary: Sara Rampazzi is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.1/5 in overall quality and 3.6/5 in difficulty across 7 review(s). 43% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Professor assigns unrealistic PhD projects with no resources, expecting completion in 3 months and it is 40% weightage, unclear grading. "Bonus" points added to total score, not as bonus. Frustrating experience, top it off with strong accent.

Review-CIS5370 (Computer Information Security)
All theory, minimal practical work except project.
Choose project carefully; not always related to CS.
Harsh Grading, small mistakes cost a lot.
Projects can be overwhelming.
Doesn't recognize your efforts; Results matter more than actual effort.
Extra credit-attendance.
Read slides, quizzes for exams

Prof has tons of cool research. She very clearly is passionate about the subject and can explain things well, but her speaking voice is very monotone, which sometimes makes things dull. Cool class tho

The grade is a few quizzes + 1 semester-long group project. Luckily, they were all very easy or graded leniently.

Much easier than her other class

name: Mohammad Al-Saad
summary: Mohammad Al-Saad is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 4.4/5 in overall quality and 2/5 in difficulty across 27 review(s). 78% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Amazing professor. He holds review sessions outside of class before our exams to make sure students understand the content they will be tested on. He really cares about students learning and understanding the content.

Ehhh, he just read off slides and the content was not hard at all. However this guy really cares about his students. Would 100% take again. You could tell he was sad students did not show up. He wants everyone to succeed, "the more A's the better". Very doable without attending lecture. But go! Make him happy, we need more like him.

Good lectures; if you attend/watch, you will absolutely pass the exams easily. I found it VERY effective to watch them in 2x speed, so I got the information and avoided the extra tangents. Sometimes his slides don't tell the full story but he is very thorough with drawings and diagrams in class.

name: Neha Rani
summary: Neha Rani is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.4/5 in overall quality and 1.8/5 in difficulty across 20 review(s). 45% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Lectures are useless when it comes to learning anything useful for software engineering. Every week there is an in-class assignment that is a complete waste of time.
The lectures didn't really add much, and most of what you learn comes from whatever tech you end up using for the semester project

Her lectures were completely a waste of time and she looked like she didn't want to be teaching. We were not prepared by her lectures or the TA's discussion to create the main group project. All the documentation assignments took away from our time teaching ourselves full-stack dev and it seemed like busy work. This class was so frustrating.

name: Jaime Ruiz
summary: Jaime Ruiz is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.4/5 in overall quality and 3.5/5 in difficulty across 13 review(s). 23% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Mediocre lecturer with extremely sloppy handwriting that is hard to read. He was at a conference for a week before a really tough quiz and left us with nearly no resources to study and successfully complete the homework. Our class's averages are always disturbingly lower than that of the other professor's class. Only pro is that he is lenient.

UNREASONABLE CLASS AND PROFESSOR!!

The exam content is so vast, and even going to lectures doesn't help. The TAs are so unhelpful and straight up deny help. 

VERY cool concept and topics, but the way this prof is operating, makes it so unmanageable.

Wish he atleast fixed up his own slides before harshly grading students for "bad" presentations

Super interesting material, but the rubric for the projects was so unclear at times. The project is so time consuming but it's only worth 30% of the grade while two exams being worth 55% of the grade is ridiculous for a class that should be mainly project based. Prof seemed to care more about students showing up to lecture than actually learning.

name: James Fairbanks
summary: James Fairbanks is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.5/5 in overall quality and 4.2/5 in difficulty across 10 review(s). 20% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: The most horrible experience I've had. The lectures dont make any sense and textbook isnt much better. Explains in HEAVY linear algebra terms which is not a prereq so to a surprise to no one everyone was confised asf. Nice guy but everything that comes out his mouth makes no sense. Even admitted that a few weeks ago the highest grade was like a 50%

I think he's never taught undergrad, because he makes no sense. If you can take another professor, you should. His lectures go off on tangents that don't apply to the content. He's a nice guy, but a horrible lecturer. Also, he takes forever to grade. Participation is mandatory but he doesn't tell you that until halfway into the semester. Don't take

MidSem review. Class takes twice the time you think. Attendance is required so you sit in class confused the entire time, then you have to go home to actually teach yourself to make sense of the lecture. Has online options (begrudgingly) but he's impossible to reach through those channels. 1st time teaching this class so hes disorganized.

name: Randi Faris
summary: Randi Faris is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 1.4/5 in overall quality and 4.4/5 in difficulty across 14 review(s). 14% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Bad teacher, bad course. The same complaints have been going on since 2020 and still nothing has been done to improve anything. Enough people complained this semester and only now, a month before finals, will she finally allow us to see what we get wrong on quizzes moving forward. The damage is done. I paid for an education, not whatever this is.

I'm sorry that I took this course. What a waste of time and I'll have to retake it anyway. She doesn't teach anything but tears your grades apart over every little detail, even though she doesn't explain most of them. The quizzes & assignments she puts out all have errors/typos in them, and she likes to regrade after the autograder to deduct points

What make her a terrible teacher is that she does not leave margin for error, she heavily relies on Grade scope to grade student assignments and what you get on grade scope you get on your assignment grade but most time's grade scope has a different way of looking at student code like Faris shows in canvas I feel like satisfies  grade scope.

name: Alin Dobra
summary: Alin Dobra is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.8/5 in overall quality and 2.7/5 in difficulty across 58 review(s). 69% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Semester is 1 group project that you have to teach yourself through Googling and SO. Dobra does this as a side hustle and prefers lecturing about incoherent nonsense and personal stories over teaching anything related to the required coding. What you learn from him is general industry advice based on common sense but he thinks he's Einstein for it.
Always willing to share his knowledge. Amazing lectures with lots of interesting examples. Projects will help a lot in learning.

People love this professor so this might be a not popular opinion, but I do not get this class(Distributed OS). There are no exams, there are 5 huge projects that are in some new language every semester, sometimes F#, in my case Erlang, and he doesn't help with it, he just tells you to do the work and that's it. People just like it due to no exams.

name: Alireza Entezari
summary: Alireza Entezari is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.9/5 in overall quality and 3.3/5 in difficulty across 43 review(s). 53% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: I'm a straight A student. I read the book carefully, watch all lectures, and study hard for my exams. This is the worst class I have ever taken, in every regard. 4 exams. 20-20-20-40 for your final grade. Class avg on the first 3 was a C-. Class avg on the final was 50%. Prof seems to know his stuff, but is not an effective teacher imo.

Straight A occasional B student and was lucky to not fail. Worst class I've ever taken. Only graded on 4 exams no drops. Do bad on one exam too bad. Last 2 exams are past drop off date, 60% of your grade is determined in last 3 weeks. Class average on final exam was 52%, average grade being a B- and that is with a huge grade curve. Worst teacher

Low quality learning material and poor grading criteria. Class average before final was around 70% after a lot of students dropped. The final was a disaster. No communication on the final grades. This is a core class which you have to take so this review is just to make you guys aware about the level of stress you'll need to prepare for. Good luck!

name: Daisy Zhe Wang
summary: Daisy Zhe Wang is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.2/5 in overall quality and 3.4/5 in difficulty across 22 review(s). 28% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Nice professor, she has a clear interest and investment in the subject matter. May give difficult projects and tests, but the material is rarely mundane and sometimes pretty interesting. Getting an A will be tough, but passing the class should not be.

She's pretty bad. Grading was often arbitrary and defined cutoffs were never established. Lectures were taught at a high theoretical level, while assignments were all program-based. This disconnection was the worst thing about this course. The assumption was that every student knew every function of every data science-related library already.

While the projects are great and tough. Credit distribution is bad and unfairly done. Lectures are not engaging. Grading criteria is very unclear and projects get around 6-7 members leading to obvious complaints.

name: Scott Siegel
summary: Scott Siegel is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 3.3/5 in overall quality and 2/5 in difficulty across 6 review(s). 50% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Dr. Siegel was pretty great. The content at times is a bit obscure, so it is a bit difficult for him to explain, but he does a good job. There were only 4-5 HW assignments, 3 at home quizzes, 1 midterm exam, and a final project that is mostly done by AI. Good introduction to Python and highly recommend.

He is knowledgeable in what he is teaching about and it is clear he is learning the ropes a little but the course itself is premature and they only recently switched from MATLAB to Python so it makes sense that the lecture content would be a little bit rough in that regard. The class is easy if you follow along with the exercises. He's very nice.

Really nice guy and encourages students who are interested. However, lectures often feel like rambling and if your not good at python its sometimes hard too follow, especially because he can go too fast during his lectures. Homeworks and quizzes were not hard, but he didn't give lots of instruction for final project so I had no idea what to do.

name: Jean Louis
summary: Jean Louis is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.7/5 in overall quality and 3/5 in difficulty across 3 review(s). 33% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Weekly material is rushed as he tries to compress surface level information on an entire new field into 2 lectures every week. Assignments are time consuming and unrelated to class material. Be ready to get graded based on how he likes your project personally and not according to any rubric. Overall, there are just better alternatives to this class

This "Full Stack IoT Dev" class was 95% an attempt at full stack and 5% at IoT.

I say attempt because each week was just a haphazard _attempt_ at teaching all of software dev or all of AI in one lecture.

Think of this class as a worse version of SWE + some baby circuits sprinkled in.

Assignments are posted with little notice or instruction.

This class is really valuable for CS majors, very cool assignments. He is a harsh grader so do not expect your grade to be perfect. There is a lot of hard circuits the entire time but it is worth it for the software assignments. you need to start the circuit assignments the day they come out to be able to get OH help.

name: Christan Grant
summary: Christan Grant is a Computer Science professor at University of Florida. Rated 2.2/5 in overall quality and 3.4/5 in difficulty across 14 review(s). 40% of students would take them again.

top_3_comments.comment: Had Dr. Grant for OS. Considering it was his first time teaching that class, he was alright. He definitely could improve on being more clear about what will be on and exam/be more clear on what type of curving style he does, but rather than that, he was pretty good.

I have professor Grant he is one of the best professors I've ever had in my college experience. Very knowledgeable and the work is not that bad he really helped me learn the subject. In my humble opinion I think everybody should take his class

Poor lecturer: just plain doesn't explain things well, doesn't seem to understand most concepts himself.

Homework assignments were terrible: tedious without teaching much.

Bad at answering questions, he would just explain the overall assignment/concept in response to specific questions.

Lazy: exams were made from questions that students wrote.
